title: Impatiens oliveri
taxonomy: Impatiens oliveri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Balsamaceae. It belongs to the genus Impatiens, which consists of around 1,000 species of plants. Impatiens oliveri is a type of balsam or jewelweed, commonly found in New Guinea. The plant is native to the region and is known for its vibrant flowers and delicate foliage. Its classification also places it in the order Ericales, a group of flowering plants that also includes heath and heather species.
